tumor.summary: Right colon high grade mixed neuroendocrine and non-neuroendocrine neoplasm (MiNEN), Ki-67 about 84 percent, mismatch repair deficient (MLH1 and PMS2 loss) and BRAF V600E.

summary.patient: This tumor was read at several biological levels at once. It has a repair defect (MSI-high, from loss of the MLH1 gene) that unlocks immunotherapy, and separately a BRAF V600E mutation. In this real case the immunotherapy produced a durable remission. Anveshar matched these options from the tumor biology, even though the cancer began in the colon rather than the rectum.
summary.clinician: Four variant classes were interpreted together: an SNV hotspot (BRAF V600E), an epigenetic silencing event (MLH1 loss producing sporadic dMMR), the derived composite MSI-H biomarker, and the expected mismatch repair mutational signature as orthogonal support. The decisive, guideline backed match is PD-1 blockade for MSI-H or dMMR (pembrolizumab, KEYNOTE-158 and first line KEYNOTE-177), consistent with the reported durable remission; BRAF V600E is a secondary or backup rationale, and in colorectal lineage the preferred regimen is encorafenib plus cetuximab (BEACON), not BRAF monotherapy.
summary.researcher: A hypermutated dMMR genome in which an epigenetic event (MLH1 silencing) is interpreted as functionally equivalent to biallelic loss and bridges to the tumor-agnostic MSI-H immunotherapy biomarker. BRAF V600E occurring alongside MLH1 loss dMMR argues for sporadic rather than Lynch origin. The profile also demonstrates the VUS reclassification workflow: a Tier III missense variant would be triaged by AlphaMissense and a splice or noncoding variant by Evo 2, routed to functional confirmation and never promoted to a clinical tier on a prediction alone.

alterations.actionability: OncoKB Level 1; ESCAT I-C (tumor-agnostic); AMP/ASCO/CAP Tier I
alterations.note: The decisive actionable finding. It predicts benefit from PD-1 blockade as a tumor-agnostic indication (pembrolizumab, KEYNOTE-158) and is first line for MSI-H or dMMR colorectal cancer (KEYNOTE-177). Consistent with the reported durable remission on pembrolizumab.

alterations.variant: Loss of MLH1 and PMS2 protein by IHC; MSH2 and MSH6 intact
alterations.pathogenicity: dMMR by IHC (Lynch excluded by germline testing)
alterations.note: An epigenetic silencing event interpreted as functionally equivalent to biallelic loss of function, and the mechanistic source of the MSI-H immunotherapy biomarker. The promoter methylation assay was not separately reported, so the methylation mechanism is inferred (UNVERIFIED); the dMMR phenotype itself is verified by immunohistochemistry.

alterations.actionability: OncoKB Level 1 (CRC); ESCAT I-B; AMP/ASCO/CAP Tier I
alterations.pathogenicity: ClinVar VCV000013961: Oncogenic, somatic Tier I (Strong)
alterations.note: Activating MAPK driver. In colorectal lineage the preferred regimen is encorafenib plus cetuximab (BRAF plus EGFR blockade, BEACON CRC), not BRAF monotherapy, because BRAF monotherapy triggers feedback EGFR reactivation. Secondary to the MSI-H immunotherapy indication in this patient.

alterations.variant: Illustrative Tier III missense VUS, not a real variant in this case
alterations.actionability: Not assignable until reclassified (ESCAT Tier X)
alterations.note: Method demonstration only. A Tier III missense VUS would be scored by AlphaMissense (Cheng 2023) and a splice or noncoding VUS by Evo 2 (Brixi 2025) zero shot; a damaging prediction routes the variant to functional confirmation and is never promoted to a clinical tier on its own. No numeric score is asserted because none is published for this case.
